posts.author: code_croupier (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I’ve been working on a small project to improve my own time tracking workflow with monday.com, and I thought others might find it useful too. It’s a Chrome extension called monday.watch, and it aims to make starting and stopping timers a bit faster and less disruptive. You can check it out here https://monday.watch

The idea is simple: bring the core time tracking functionality into the browser extension panel. This way, you always see if the timetracking is running, the time itself plus you don’t have to switch tabs or navigate within monday.com just to clock in or out. For me, it’s cut down the time it takes to start/pause a timer considerably (classic route - open monday, wait for it to load, find the timer button, from ~7 seconds to ~1.5 seconds, in my testing).

It’s still in its early stages (alpha, really), but the basic functionality is there. You can install it for free from the Chrome Web Store: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/mondaywatch/ljalcnfcfggecnmgcnlmgdajipnmokcb

I’m really keen to get some feedback from anyone who’s willing to try it out. If you have any thoughts, good or bad, please let me know! You can reach me at mondaywatchapp@gmail.com or on X (formerly Twitter) at @codecroupier. I’m open to suggestions and feature requests.

Thanks!
